data.description:
Goal:
Replace the placeholder `assets/demo.cast` (committed by K-46) with a real
asciinema recording so the README's demo link plays an actual session
instead of comment-only stub.

Verification:
1. `wc -c assets/demo.cast` is non-trivial (>1 KiB) and the file's first
   line is asciinema's standard JSON header `{"version": 2, ...}`, not the
   `#` placeholder comments.
2. `asciinema play assets/demo.cast` (or the asciinema-player web embed)
   produces a clean session: init -> recipient add --bootstrap -> set with
   provision -> list -> render to /dev/null. Total runtime under ~30s.
3. No real secret values appear on screen: every `set` flow uses
   `provision` piped to `set --origin=provision`, and `render` redirects
   to /dev/null.
4. README's "Demo" subsection (anchor in K-46) renders the cast (either
   via asciinema.org link or asciinema-player snippet pointed at the
   committed file). One-line caption explains what's happening.

Constraints:
- Knot ref: K-49; follow-up to K-46.
- Files: assets/demo.cast (overwrite placeholder), README.md (caption).
- Notes: Operator records locally with `asciinema rec assets/demo.cast bash scripts/demo.sh`. The `make demo` target prints the exact command. Don't fake a cast file by hand-editing JSON — re-record if the timing is wrong.
